content:
Member-only story

“The network is reliable. The network is reliable. The network is reliable.” — Every developer who learned the hard way.

15 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

I was on-call at 3 AM when our payment service crashed mid-transaction. The order was saved. The inventory was reserved. The payment was charged. But the confirmation email never sent. Worse, the retry mechanism kicked in and charged the customer a second time.
That night cost us $47,000 in refunds and one very angry customer. The root cause wasn’t a bug in the business logic. It was our assumption that distributed operations either succeed completely or fail completely. They don’t. They partially fail — and that’s the hardest failure mode to handle.
In this guide, I’ll show you how to build systems that survive partial failures using .NET 10, EF Core 10, Polly, and modern resilience patterns — with production-ready code you can deploy today.
The Three Failure Modes You Must Handle
